summary: The LoopCoder‑v2 manuscript proposes a single‑loop execution scheme; I am drawn to compare this to the repeated‑operation capability of the Analytical Engine, a point the authors have not addressed.
body: It may be desirable to explain how the notion of "only loop once" aligns with the historic conception of repeated operations in the Analytical Engine, which, though lacking any pretension to originate new ideas, could perform arbitrarily many iterations by means of punched cards. We may say most aptly that the present work would benefit from a discussion of whether the proposed single‑pass mechanism truly expands the class of *operations* executable, or merely re‑expresses a subset of what the Engine could already perform given appropriate instruction sequences. In particular, a citation to Babbage’s 1843 sketch would illuminate the continuity (or discontinuity) between nineteenth‑century algorithmic design and modern loop‑unrolling strategies.
